title: Problem 848 Structural Lift Checklist

sourceArtifacts.exactBaseCertificateMeaning: bookkeeping surface; raw 1..40500 packet is local and ignored

northStar: Turn the bounded mixed-base structural verifier into an all-N proof lane by proving the cross-side matching reduction, then the p=13 and p=17 margin lifts, while keeping exact finite coverage separate.

currentFocus.why: The C graph-reduction packet is theorem-facing through C6, and D1 now extracts the bounded matching target from every current exact mixed-base threatening-outsider row. The next decisive lift is the symbolic p=13 matching lower bound, which is the tightest current D-lane family.

completionRule: Each step is complete only when it has a statement, hypotheses, proof sketch or proof artifact, falsifier boundary, dependencies, and the next downstream step updated.

phases.title: Counting Language
phases.goal: Turn verifier counts into exact residue-count and floor-function terms.

steps.statement: # {m <= N : m == r mod q} = floor((N - r) / q) + 1 when N >= r, else 0.
steps.falsifierBoundary: A boundary case with r <= 0, r > q, or N < r is mishandled.

steps.statement: |B7(N)| and |B18(N)| are residue counts modulo 25, and candidateSize(N) is their maximum.
steps.falsifierBoundary: The formula disagrees with the verifier's base-side candidate counts.

steps.statement: For each witness prime p == 1 mod 4, n^2 + 1 == 0 mod p^2 has two Hensel-lifted root progressions, counted by residue-count formulas.
steps.falsifierBoundary: A root progression fails the n^2 + 1 == 0 mod p^2 check or the count disagrees with the verifier.

steps.statement: vMax(N,p) = max(rawPlus(N,p), rawMinus(N,p)).
steps.falsifierBoundary: The verifier uses a different witness-count maximum.

steps.statement: rGreater(N,p) is the sum of active root-event counts from witness-prime blocks q > p.
steps.falsifierBoundary: A higher-prime event is double-counted, omitted, or ordered differently from the verifier.

phases.title: Compatibility Language
phases.goal: Convert outsider/base compatibility into congruence conditions.

steps.statement: For fixed outsider x and base b, compatibility is witnessed by some prime square q^2 dividing x*b + 1.
steps.falsifierBoundary: The compatibility definition diverges from squarefree/non-squarefree semantics.

steps.statement: If gcd(x,q)=1, then q^2 | x*b + 1 iff b == -x^{-1} mod q^2.
steps.falsifierBoundary: A non-invertible x modulo q^2 is treated as invertible.

steps.statement: If gcd(x,q) != 1, compatibility is either impossible or recorded as a finite exception boundary before using inverse formulas.
steps.falsifierBoundary: A noncoprime case creates a compatibility family not covered by the stated exception boundary.

steps.statement: For fixed x and N, the compatible base set is the union of all base-side residue hits produced by active square witnesses.
steps.falsifierBoundary: A compatible base vertex is missing from the union or an incompatible vertex is included.

phases.title: Mixed-Base Graph Reduction
phases.goal: Prove the graph identity used by the exact mixed-base verifier.

steps.statement: Inside one principal base side, compatible base vertices form a clique for the fixed outsider graph considered by the verifier.
steps.falsifierBoundary: Two same-side compatible vertices fail the pair compatibility relation used by the verifier.

steps.statement: For fixed outsider x, the compatible mixed-base graph is one clique on B7-compatible vertices, one clique on B18-compatible vertices, plus arbitrary cross edges.
steps.falsifierBoundary: The graph has a same-side missing edge or an extra vertex outside the two compatible sides.

steps.statement: Define H_{x,N} as the bipartite graph whose edges are precisely missing cross edges between compatible B7 and B18 vertices.
steps.falsifierBoundary: A cross edge is assigned the wrong sign between compatibility and missing-cross membership.

steps.statement: A mixed-base clique equals all compatible vertices minus a vertex cover of H_{x,N}; hence max clique size is |L| + |R| minus minimum vertex cover size.
steps.falsifierBoundary: A selected clique contains a missing cross edge or a vertex cover removes more vertices than necessary.

steps.statement: Because H_{x,N} is bipartite, minimum vertex cover size equals maximum matching size.
steps.falsifierBoundary: H_{x,N} is not bipartite or Konig's theorem is used outside its hypotheses.

steps.statement: sMixed(x,N) = |L| + |R| - maxMatching(H_{x,N}).
steps.falsifierBoundary: The formula disagrees with the verifier's Hopcroft-Karp mixed-clique computation on a certified row.

phases.title: Matching Lower Bounds
phases.goal: Replace Hopcroft-Karp computation with symbolic matching constructions.

steps.statement: Extract the current residue-block matching target from all bounded exact mixed-base threatening-outsider rows: K(N,x) = |L| + |R| - strictBaseThreshold + 1, and the recorded matching saturates the smaller compatible side in every current row.
steps.falsifierBoundary: A refreshed exact mixed-base threatening-outsider row has matching below K(N,x), disagrees with the C6 formula replay, or fails the smaller-side saturation pattern. This bounded extraction is not yet the all-N symbolic matching proof.

steps.statement: For p=13 threatening rows, maxMatching(H_{x,N}) is at least the required matching bound extracted from the margin inequality.
steps.falsifierBoundary: A p=13 family row fails the claimed lower bound.

steps.statement: For p=17 threatening rows, maxMatching(H_{x,N}) is at least the required matching bound extracted from the margin inequality.
steps.falsifierBoundary: A p=17 family row fails the claimed lower bound.

steps.statement: If maxMatching(H_{x,N}) >= K(N), then sMixed(x,N) <= |L| + |R| - K(N).
steps.falsifierBoundary: The matching lower bound is substituted with the wrong inequality direction.

phases.title: Margin Lifts
phases.goal: Prove eventual positivity of the structural margins.

steps.statement: Bound sMaxMixed, vMax, dMax, and rGreater for p=13 by explicit floor-function expressions.
steps.falsifierBoundary: The symbolic bound is lower than a verifier row term.

steps.statement: candidateSize(N) - (sMixedBound13(N) + vMax13(N) + dMax13(N) + rGreater13(N)) > 0 for all N >= T13.
steps.falsifierBoundary: A residue class modulo the proof period gives nonpositive margin beyond T13.

steps.statement: Bound sMaxMixed, vMax, dMax, and rGreater for p=17 by explicit floor-function expressions.
steps.falsifierBoundary: The symbolic bound is lower than a verifier row term.

steps.statement: candidateSize(N) - (sMixedBound17(N) + vMax17(N) + dMax17(N) + rGreater17(N)) > 0 for all N >= T17.
steps.falsifierBoundary: A residue class modulo the proof period gives nonpositive margin beyond T17.

steps.statement: For witness primes outside {13,17}, the safe union-bound structural margin is eventually positive.
steps.falsifierBoundary: A non-{13,17} witness-prime block needs exact mixed-base repair beyond the finite verifier range.

phases.title: Finite Closure
phases.goal: Attach symbolic lemmas to existing finite certificates without overclaiming.

steps.statement: The local compact exact rollout reaches N <= 40500; the committed raw packet reaches N <= 10000.
steps.falsifierBoundary: The exact certificate is stale, missing, or fails rerun.

steps.statement: The full mixed-base structural verifier certifies 7307..7600 with zero mixed failures.
steps.falsifierBoundary: The full verifier artifact loses allMixedChecksPass or its parameters drift.

steps.statement: The symbolic thresholds T13, T17, and Ttail overlap the finite exact or structural certificates.
steps.falsifierBoundary: A gap remains between the finite certificate end and a symbolic threshold.

steps.statement: Exact base plus symbolic structural lift cover all positive integers in the problem claim.
steps.falsifierBoundary: A positive integer N is covered by neither exact verification nor symbolic structural lift.

recommendedNextSteps.task: Prove the symbolic p=13 lower bound that explains why maxMatching(H_{x,N}) always reaches the D1-extracted K(N,x) target in the tightest current exact-mixed family.
Keep the discharged C6 mixed-clique matching formula regression-checked against representative full-verifier rows.
For the top p=13 families, extract the residue-block pattern behind the smaller-side-saturating matchings so D2 can become a symbolic construction rather than bounded evidence.

boundary.note: This checklist records a proof plan and dependencies. It does not certify any new N by itself.
boundary.promotionRule: Only mark a step done after the statement, hypotheses, proof/falsifier boundary, and downstream dependency updates are committed.
